company: Haystack (hiring for a global consumer goods company)
title: Marketing Graduate / Brand Manager
required_skills: Bachelor's or Master's degree with strong academic results
Leadership drive and passion for Brand Management
Ability to work with diverse groups embracing creativity and innovation
Less than 2 years of work experience after university graduation (excluding internships)
matched_skills: Analytical and problem-solving skills (competitive analysis, KPI dashboards, ROI reporting)
Cross-functional influence and collaboration with diverse teams
Consumer/market insight capabilities (voice-of-customer research, market segmentation)
Product launch and GTM strategy (relevant to brand and commercial innovation responsibilities)
missing_skills: HARD DISQUALIFIER: Candidate has 4+ years of full-time post-graduation work experience; role explicitly requires fewer than 2 years — this is a graduate-entry scheme Boru is overqualified for
Explicit consumer brand management experience (background is B2B/tech product marketing, not FMCG brand management)
Consumer goods / FMCG industry background (no relevant sector experience)
core_responsibilities: Lead master plans for product innovations, commercial innovations, and marketing elements across all media channels
Develop marketing concepts and identify tactics based on consumer, shopper, and customer insights
Manage own business unit from day one, executing ideas end-to-end
Work across Commercial Operations, Brand Operations, or Sales teams based on skills and interests
Contribute to creating meaningful and sustainable brand experiences
recommended_emphasis: CAUTION: This is a graduate-entry scheme requiring less than 2 years post-graduation experience — Boru's 4+ years full-time history is a structural mismatch and may trigger automatic screening out
If applying, de-emphasize seniority and frame experience as breadth of early-career exposure rather than accumulated tenure
Highlight MBA academic excellence and strong analytical results to align with the 'strong academic results' requirement
Emphasize consumer and market insight work: voice-of-customer research, market segmentation, competitive analysis
Draw parallels between Product Marketing Manager roles and brand management: GTM strategy, product positioning, multi-market promotion (EMEA, Asia)
Highlight German (B2) as a meaningful differentiator for a Germany-based hybrid role
Frame cross-functional influence and stakeholder collaboration as evidence of meeting the 'diverse groups' and leadership potential requirements
